description: A daytime photograph of the facade of the Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building in Washington, D.C., shot from a low angle against a blue sky with white clouds. The photo is a candid architectural exterior, but it prominently documents agency-designed propaganda: on the left a very large vertical American flag is draped down the building, and on the right hangs a tall maroon/sepia-toned portrait banner of former President Theodore Roosevelt bearing a small 'AMERICA 250' anniversary mark and large white serif text reading 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST.' A state/agency flag on a pole and a cluster of white globe streetlamps are also visible, with small figures on the rooftop. This is a companion image to the wider building shot, focusing on the Theodore Roosevelt 'American Workers First' banner. Propaganda technique: monumental patriotic display associating the labor agenda with a revered historical Republican president as part of the America 250 branding. Target audience is the general public and the administration's nationalist/labor base. (Marked designed because the banner is an agency-produced propaganda graphic, though the file is a photograph of it.)
notable_text: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST. AMERICA 250. (Theodore Roosevelt banner.)
